channel.description: Storage Management Data is stored on disks that are logically divided into partitions. A partition can exist on a portion of a disk, on an entire disk, or it may span multiple disks. Each partition is accessed and managed independent of other partitions and may contain a file system or swap space. Partitioning information is stored at special disk locations that the system references at boot time. Partitions created with a combination of most tools can coexist on a single disk.
